title: Global citizenship and sustainable development education
subtitle: An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.
caption: Four countries in 2020 show varying levels of mainstreaming global citizenship and sustainable development education across four strands: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula, measured on an index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best). Colombia and India both score 1 in national education policies and student assessment; Colombia’s education curricula and teacher education fall slightly lower at 0.88 and 0.85, while India’s teacher education and education curricula register 0.95 and 0.92. Malawi matches the top scores in national education policies and student assessment at 1, with education curricula at 0.91 and teacher education at 0.9. Bulgaria’s highest score is 0.73 in teacher education, followed by 0.71 in student assessment, 0.65 in education curricula, and 0.56 in national education policies — the lowest value among all categories shown for these four countries. The bars show that three of the four countries reach the maximum index value in at least two strands, while Bulgaria’s scores cluster in the middle-to-lower range across all four.
